description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2918 (MacBook Pro 14" M3 base). Follow sequentially from Step 1. Has MagSafe + 2x USB-C + HDMI + SD Card. M3 generation uses enhanced comparator circuit: U5950 + Q5960 throttle MOSFET (replaces simple U8110 on M1 or U9810 on M2).
WARNING: A2918 (820-02757) MacBook Pro 14" M3 BASE ONLY. NOT M3 Pro/Max. SVR AON is 30A 3-phase (U5700). M3 comparator is U5950+Q5960 (NOT U8110 as on M1, NOT U9810 as on M2). Has MagSafe, HDMI, SD Card slot. Luxe backlight.
data_source: Schematic 820-02757 page references. M3 power-on sequence verified against M1/M2 topology with M3-specific comparator and throttle circuit differences.

eason_reference.source: M3 series standby circuit — enhanced comparator design with active throttle MOSFET Q5960
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC 5V via CD3217 MOSFET or MagSafe (Ext PWR pages 54-56) → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → U5200 charger IC (page 52), support (page 53). R5215/R5216 → CHGR_AUX_DET → U5200 detect
3. U5200 → Q5230/Q5240 → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). CHGR_EN_MVR → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → U5700
4. U5700 3-phase SVR AON (30A) → Q5800/Q5820/Q5840 → L5800/L5820/L5840 → PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN

5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → U5950 comparator + Q5960 throttle MOSFET. PPBUS_AON via divider → U5950 same input. PP3V8_AON → U5950 opposite input. When same>opposite, U5950 drives Q5960 gate → PMU voltage detect. M3 adds active throttle: Q5960 can limit current if comparator detects marginal conditions.
6. MPMU → all master rails + PP3V3_AON, PP1V8_AON. SPMU → slave rails (pages 77-80 SPMU, 81-87 MPMU)
7. M3 hard start (24MHz + 32.768KHz crystals + PMU_RESET_L). M3 reads ROM, I2C to CD3217
8. M3 → MPMU → P5VS2_PWR_EN → PP5V_S2 via TPS62130 (page 122). AFTER hard start.
9. CD3217 reads shared ROM, PD via CC → charger boosts to 20V

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR or post-SVR. M3 uses U5950+Q5960 instead of simple U8110 jumper.
steps: 1. Inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN (use 4.3V if testing with display attached)
2. Force U5950 comparator output high, OR jumper around Q5960 throttle MOSFET to bypass active throttle
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is FRONT END — charger IC, CD3217, fuses, MagSafe path, boost circuit. Check Steps 1-2.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is BACK END — PMU, SoC power, clocks, ROM. Check Steps 3+.

q5960_throttle.description: M3-specific: Q5960 active throttle MOSFET diagnostic. If board throttles severely under load but doesn't crash, Q5960 may be partially failed — stuck in partial conduction.
q5960_throttle.symptom: Board boots but throttles aggressively, thermal throttle without heat, performance capped well below normal M3 levels
q5960_throttle.test: Measure Q5960 gate voltage under load. Should swing fully high when comparator satisfied. If gate voltage is marginal (1.5-2.5V instead of >3V), Q5960 is suspect.
q5960_throttle.fix: Replace Q5960 throttle MOSFET. Check U5950 comparator divider resistors for drift.

pbus_efuse.description: M3 Pro 14" may have PBUS E-Fuse protection on certain power paths. If PPBUS_AON is intermittent, check E-Fuse state.
pbus_efuse.test: Measure resistance across E-Fuse. Should be <50 milliohms. If open or high resistance, E-Fuse has blown.
pbus_efuse.fix: Replace E-Fuse or jumper for diagnostic purposes only. Identify downstream short that caused blow.

tree.probe: Bulk capacitors near U5200 charger IC output (page 52). Also check MagSafe path (Ext PWR pages 54-56).

check_sequence: Check PPVBUS_USBC at USB-C connector pins (pages 66-77) — should have 5V or 20V from adapter
Check MagSafe input path (Ext PWR pages 54-56) — MagSafe connector and protection circuit
Check CD3217 port controllers for USB-C ports (USB-C pages 66-77)
Check charger IC U5200 (page 52) gate drives and output FETs
Check charger support circuitry (page 53)
Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector — charger needs battery detect
Check fuses in PPDCIN path — if blown, short downstream
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs

tree.probe: PP3V8_AON output caps near U5700 SVR AON controller (3V8 AON pages 57-59).

check_sequence: Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN enable signal (page 57)
Check P3V8AON_FAULT_L (active low fault, page 57) — if low, U5700 reports fault
Diode mode on PP3V8_AON output caps — check for short on VDDMAIN branch
Check 3-phase driver FETs: Q5800 (DRVL1 via L5800), Q5820 (DRVL2 via L5820), Q5840 (DRVL3 via L5840)

CRITICAL M3: Check U5950 comparator + Q5960 throttle MOSFET (pages 57-59). PPBUS_AON via divider → U5950 same input. PP3V8_AON → U5950 opposite input. U5950 drives Q5960 gate. If U5950 or Q5960 failed, PMU voltage detect will not trigger.
Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN current sensing circuit (page 58-59)
Thermal cam on U5700, phase FETs Q5800/Q5820/Q5840, and Q5960 at low voltage (5V current-limited)
SHORTCUT: Try forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + force U5950 output high or jumper Q5960 + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: External AON LDO outputs. PP3V3_AON and PP1V8_AON near PMU area.

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700/U5950/Q5960
If PP3V3_AON missing: check LDO controller input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V8_AON missing: check LDO controller input/output, diode mode on output cap
Thermal cam on LDO area for hot component

tree.probe: Slave PMU buck output caps (SPMU pages 77-80: Input PWR page 77, Bulk page 78, BSTLQ/LDO page 79, SW/GPIO/Aliases page 80).

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to SPMU (page 77 — Input PWR)
Check SPMU bulk capacitors and input power (page 78)
Check SPMU BSTLQ / LDO outputs (page 79)
Check SPMU SW / GPIO / Aliases (page 80)
Diode mode on individual buck output cap banks
Thermal cam on SPMU area — if hot, internal short or downstream loading
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing PMU

tree.probe: Master PMU buck output caps (MPMU pages 81-87: Bucks page 81-82, LDO page 83, Misc page 84, GPIO page 85-86, Support page 87). Also PMU Support page 88.
tree.expect: Multiple MPMU buck rails present. Board should have power button response.

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to MPMU (page 81)
CRITICAL M3: Check U5950 comparator output → Q5960 throttle → PMU voltage detect chain. Unlike M1 (U8110 jumper pins 4+5), M3 requires forcing U5950 output or bypassing Q5960.
Check 32.768KHz crystal — MPMU needs this clock to start
Check PMU_ONOFF_L power button signal
Check MPMU buck enables (pages 81-82)
Check MPMU LDO outputs (page 83)
Check MPMU GPIO states (pages 85-86)
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output
Check PMU Support circuitry (page 88)
Thermal cam on MPMU area
If LDO outputs missing: check individual LDO enables on page 83

tree.probe: TPS62130 output (page 122). Also check External Switches (page 128).

check_sequence: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal (page 122) — gated by PMU GPIO
Check TPS62130 input PPBUS_AON
Check TPS62130 output inductor and sense resistor
Check External Switches (page 128) for downstream load switch faults
Diode mode on PP5V_S2 output caps for shorts downstream
Thermal cam on TPS62130 area

tree.probe: SoC power pages 23-30. PCPU, ECPU, GPU, DDR domains from PMU bucks.
tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present at SoC pins. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault or SPI ROM fault
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash — if SoC can't read firmware, no boot
Check secure element
Check SoC ground connections — open ground can cause no-boot
Check Storage power (pages 89-91) — SoC needs NAND for boot
If all power present but no Apple logo: try DFU mode via USB-C

substeps.probe: Display power sequencer and Luxe backlight (pages 94-96)
substeps.note: A2918 uses Luxe backlight controller. Check backlight boost circuit on pages 94-96.
2x USB-C ports. CD3217 controllers. Check SPI ROM for PD negotiation.
MagSafe shares charger path with USB-C. Check MagSafe connector, protection circuit, and current sense.
Check HDMI level shifters and ESD protection.
Check SD card slot connector and controller IC.
M3 NAND is paired to SoC. If NAND fault confirmed, board may be unrepairable without donor SoC.
Check speaker amps, codec IC, headphone jack circuit.
Check keyboard backlight, membrane ZIF connector, IOX isolation.
